pattern: Checking every base from 2 to n-2 reveals that base n-2 always represents n as the two-digit, non-palindromic form, so no qualifying integer ever exists.
hints: Write out n in a few small bases and look at what its representation is in the largest allowed base.
In base n-2, the value n is just one group of (n-2) plus 2, giving a two-digit string whose digits differ.
Because the base n-2 representation of n is always two distinct digits, it is never a palindrome, so the strict condition fails for every n in range.

edge_inputs.description: value with a palindromic binary form still fails overall

stress.description: random values across the allowed range
